briefings.2026-05-10: Several European climbing gyms made headlines with new openings and developments this week. In France, Upzone 360 opened its doors in Rodez with an innovative rotating concept, while the Altissimo XXL climbing hall was inaugurated in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne. Belgium saw the launch of Bon Matin in Etterbeek, combining a climbing gym with a neighborhood cafÃ© concept. Meanwhile in Germany, a unique church conversion project is underway in WÃ¼rzburg where the St. Andreas church is being transformed into a climbing hall by Rock Inn operators.
briefings.2026-05-11: A church in Würzburg, Germany has been converted into a climbing gym, representing a growing trend of repurposing religious buildings for recreational use across Europe. This adaptive reuse provides new life for historic structures while meeting demand for climbing facilities.

stories.title: Church converted to climbing gym in Würzburg: painful farewell for parish, but opportunity for building
stories.summary: A church in Würzburg, Germany has been converted into a climbing gym. The conversion represents a painful goodbye for the local parish but provides a new lease on life for the historic building, following a trend of repurposing churches for recreational use.

stories.title: Upzone 360: New rotating bloc climbing gym opens in Rodez
stories.summary: A new bouldering gym called Upzone 360 has opened in Rodez, Aveyron, featuring an innovative rotating concept designed to keep the experience dynamic and accessible to all audiences. The facility opened on April 25, 2026, at 19 avenue Amans Rodat.

stories.title: XXL climbing gym Altissimo inaugurated in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne
stories.summary: The Altissimo climbing gym in Saint-Geours-de-Maremne was officially inaugurated on Saturday April 25, 2026, in the presence of local officials. The facility is described as a giant climbing hall serving the Landes region.

stories.title: Bon Matin combines neighborhood cafÃ© with climbing gym in Etterbeek
stories.summary: Located in a former industrial hangar in Etterbeek, Bon Matin is a hybrid venue combining a neighborhood cafÃ© with a climbing gym. The unique concept has been open since January and allows visitors to climb and then relax with food and drinks in the same space.

stories.title: WÃ¼rzburg church St. Andreas to become climbing hall
stories.summary: The Catholic church St. Andreas in WÃ¼rzburg's Sanderau district is being converted into a climbing and bouldering hall by the operators of Rock Inn. The WÃ¼rzburg diocese has agreed to the unusual conversion project, which will create the first climbing church in Bavaria.

stories.title: Montois climbing gym Face Nord fails to reach crowdfunding goals
stories.summary: The Face Nord climbing gym in Mons, Belgium, has failed to reach its crowdfunding objectives and must postpone its expansion project. The campaign, which closes on April 19, did not attract sufficient community support according to the disappointed owner.

stories.title: Bimano bouldering hall in Solothurn faces financial pressure
stories.summary: The Bimano bouldering hall in Solothurn, Switzerland is experiencing financial difficulties. The facility, operated by Jasmin Blaser, Wolfgang Antz and Jano BÃ¶hlen, is struggling with economic pressures despite the initial enthusiasm for bringing a climbing gym to the city.
